title: Simple Hanging Plant Stand (Just 2x4s)
summary: A three-piece 2x4 A-frame stand that holds a hanging plant basket at head height — nothing but two legs and a crossbar, six cuts total.
description:
This is about as simple as an outdoor plant stand gets: two 2x4 legs cross near the top in an A-frame, joined by a crossbar that also serves as the mounting point for a plant hook. Screw the two legs together where they cross with self-tapping star bit screws, then attach the crossbar between them near the top.

**The plant hook and hanging basket are what actually complete this project** — pick a hook rated for the weight of a fully watered basket, since a small stand like this has less mass to resist tipping than a heavier piece would. Set the legs wide enough at the base for real stability before anything gets hung from it.

Finish with an exterior penetrating stain, since this stand lives outdoors and plain paint will weather and chip faster on end-grain-heavy 2x4 joints like these.

steps.title: Cross the legs
steps.body: Screw the two leg pairs together where they cross near the top to form the A-frame.

steps.title: Attach the crossbar
steps.body: Attach the crossbar between the legs near the top, sized to also serve as the plant hook mounting point.

steps.title: Add the hook and finish
steps.body: Mount the plant hook to the crossbar, sand the stand, and apply exterior penetrating stain.

steps.title: Hang the basket
steps.body: Hang the plant basket from the hook.
